covenants.trustee: U.S. BANK TRUST COMPANY, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Not addressed. This convertible note indenture contains no concept of "Restricted" or "Unrestricted Subsidiaries" and no subsidiary-designation mechanic in the provided text.
covenants.anchor_clause: "7.00% Convertible Senior Notes due 2031" — This is an unsecured convertible senior note indenture that contains no maintenance financial covenants, no negative covenants restricting debt, liens, restricted payments or asset transfers, and no unrestricted-subsidiary machinery; the operative Holder protections are limited to the conversion mechanics (Article 5) and the optional repurchase right (Section 4.04). A distressed analyst should note the near-total absence of incurrence or lien covenants: the Notes rely on structural/contractual seniority in name only ("Senior"), with the amendment provisions in Article 8 governing sacred rights.

lme_vectors.rationale: The indenture contains no lien covenant, no debt-subordination or priming restriction, and no sacred-rights provision addressing collateral or payment priority in the provided text; the Notes are unsecured senior obligations with no anti-priming protection.
lme_vectors.basis: No negative covenants restricting incurrence of secured or priming debt appear; amendment provisions (Article 8) are the only Holder-protection mechanic shown.

The specific designation mechanics, conditions and caps were not included in the excerpt provided, so the degree of flexibility cannot be fully assessed from this text.
covenants.anchor_clause: "Liquidity" shall mean, on any date of determination, an amount equal to the sum of, without duplication, (a) Availability (as defined in the ABL Credit Agreement as in effect on the Effective Date) as of such date, plus (b) the aggregate amount of unrestricted cash and unrestricted Permitted Investments of the Borrower and the Restricted Subsidiaries ... provided that the aggregate amount of unrestricted cash and unrestricted Permitted Investments of Foreign Subsidiaries shall not at any time exceed 25% of all Liquidity.

lme_vectors.rationale: The text carries the machinery for IP dropdowns (Material IP, Material IP Subsidiary, Unrestricted Subsidiary, and an Excluded Subsidiary limb for IP-only subsidiaries) but the operative transfer restriction / J.Crew blocker was not included in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: 'Material IP' and 'Material IP Subsidiary' are defined; the Excluded Subsidiary definition includes a subsidiary that 'does not own any assets ... that are not Intellectual Property'; Investments definition values property transferred to/from an Unrestricted Subsidiary at fair market value.
lme_vectors.quote: that does not own any assets (other than de minimis assets) that are not Intellectual Property, so long as such subsidiary is not an obligor in respect of any Permitted Additional Indebtedness

lme_vectors.rationale: The text references incremental/additional debt concepts (Permitted Additional Indebtedness, Permitted Refinancing sized to refinanced principal plus premium/fees, Permitted Convertible Notes under Sections 6.01(s)/(t)) but the incremental facility sizing and the operative Section 6.01 debt basket schedule were not included.
lme_vectors.basis: Permitted Refinancing capped at refinanced principal plus accrued interest/premium/fees; Permitted Convertible Notes tied to Section 6.01(s)/(t); 'Permitted Additional Indebtedness' referenced in the Excluded Subsidiary IP limb.
lme_vectors.quote: has an aggregate outstanding principal amount not greater than the aggregate principal amount of the Debt being refinanced or extended plus an amount equal to accrued and unpaid interest and any premium thereon

lme_vectors.rationale: The excerpt does not include the voting/amendment (sacred rights) or pro rata sharing provisions needed to assess uptier/priming protection.
lme_vectors.basis: No amendment, sacred-rights, or pro rata sharing provisions appear in the provided text; only definitions and Permitted Encumbrances/Liens definitions are present.
lme_vectors.quote: Section 9.24. Release of Liens and Guarantees

lme_vectors.rationale: The operative restricted payments covenant (Section 6.x) is not in the excerpt; only definitional building blocks (Permitted Payee, Permitted Party, Disqualified Equity Interests) appear, so RP capacity cannot be assessed.
lme_vectors.basis: 'Permitted Payee' and 'Permitted Party' are defined but no restricted-payment basket or builder-basket text is included.
lme_vectors.quote: "Permitted Payee" means, collectively (i) any future, current or former officer, director, manager, member ... of the Borrower, any Subsidiary or any direct or indirect parent company thereof

citation.quote: "Liquidity" shall mean, on any date of determination, an amount equal to the sum of, without duplication, (a) Availability (as defined in the ABL Credit Agreement as in effect on the Effective Date) as of such date, plus (b) the aggregate amount of unrestricted cash and unrestricted Permitted Investments of the Borrower and the Restricted Subsidiaries ... provided that the aggregate amount of unrestricted cash and unrestricted Permitted Investments of Foreign Subsidiaries shall not at any time exceed 25% of all Liquidity.

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: Minimum Liquidity (financial-condition metric referenced in definitions; the maintenance covenant clause itself, Section 6.x, was not included in the excerpt)
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: Not stated in the provided text — the excerpt defines 'Liquidity' (Availability under the ABL plus unrestricted cash/Permitted Investments, with Foreign Liquidity capped at 25%) but does not include the operative covenant threshold.

total_liquidity.note: Cash and equivalents plus tagged undrawn committed credit.

annual_burn.note: Annual cash burn = negative free cash flow (OCF − capex).
